top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before December.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
5,988ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
64ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo's rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,988ft of climbing on rolling hills does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 93 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rolling hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Tempo is Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 93 miles on rolling hills. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Bike Abu Dhabi Gran Fondo’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 93 miles of rolling hills in Hazza bin Zayed Stadium, Al Ain to Sheikh Zayed Festival, Al Wathba, Abu Dhabi, UAE.
